context: Frequency:   0.1-3.0% of all gastric malignancies Age:     10-73 years; M > F Histo:  pleomorphism, hypercellularity, mitotic figures, cystic degeneration, necrosis Metastases: (a)  hematogenous to liver, lung, peritoneum; rarely to bone + soft tissue (b)  direct extension into omentum, retroperitoneum (c)  lymph nodes (rare) Location:  90% in fundus / body of stomach Site:     anterior / posterior wall; endo- / exogastric Average size:   12 cm √  intramural mass; large mass tends to be exogastric √  very frequently ulcerated √  may be pedunculated CT: √  lobulated irregular outline √  heterog
